behavior_summary: This script implements the object's 'U8P_SHORTMSH::look' (examine) handler. When the player examines the SHORTMSH object the process emits a single bark line reading "mushroom" and then the process suspends/yields.
There are no menus, branches, external calls, or global flag changes — the behavior is purely descriptive output for the examine interaction.
notable_control_flow: Linear single-step flow: start, emit bark "mushroom", then suspend/terminate the U8P_SHORTMSH::look process.
Process boundaries appear in the raw as unlabeled/Unknown nodes (an opening marker and a suspend), suggesting the routine runs once per U8P_SHORTMSH::look invocation and then yields.
open_questions: Do engine-level fast-area entry/leave triggers ever invoke this U8P_SHORTMSH::look process beyond a direct player 'examine' action? (None are referenced explicitly in the script.)
Are there localization or alternate text paths for the bark "mushroom" in other language assets? The script stores a single literal line.

main_function.name: U8P_SHORTMSH::look
main_function.purpose: Provide the short description when the player examines the object; show the single bark text "mushroom" and then suspend the process.
entry_conditions: Invoked by the engine when the player 'looks at' or examines this object (process type 0x0208).
Implicit: runs once for the U8P_SHORTMSH::look event and yields (start and suspend markers present).

function_map.name: U8P_SHORTMSH::look
function_map.role: U8P_SHORTMSH::look handler that displays the short description "mushroom"
